params.name: Wailing Wall
params.description:
On Aug 25, 70 AD, the Roman army, led by Hadrian, burned down the Second Temple of the Jews.
It is a part of the protective wall of the Second Temple of ancient Israel in Jerusalem. The entire wall is about fifty meters long and eighteen meters high, built of huge stones, and is regarded as the holiest site in Judaism. When Jewish people visit the wall, they must weep in mourning to express their sadness for the ancient temple. For thousands of years, Jewish people scattered around the world …
